title: Holiday and leave
text:
Workers might take time off work for different reasons. This could include:
holiday they're entitled to every year
unexpected time off – for example sickness or when there's an emergency
planned longer-term leave – for example when having a child
If workers are off unexpectedly or for a long time, employers should support them. Employers should also help workers to return to work smoothly.
Topics
Holiday
How to take annual leave and work out holiday pay.
Time off for sickness
What to do when a worker is ill. Sick leave. Fit notes and doctors' reports. Covid. Appointments.
Time off for care and support
How to take time off to look after others. Parental leave. Time off for bereavement.
Managing absence and returning to work
How to keep in touch and support a worker's return to work. Unauthorised absence. Trigger points.
If you like, you can tell us more about what was useful on this page. We cannot reply – so do not include any personal details, for example your email address or phone number. If you have any questions about your individual circumstances, you can contact the Acas helpline .
